meta.description: An identifiable single violent incident such as a shooting, armed attack, homicide, or armed confrontation. Each incident is a specific occurrence distinguishable by its location and date — not a violence trend, security overview, or general topic about violence. Extracted from news articles and social media.

example.description: Drive-by shooting in colonia Los Olivos left two people dead and one injured.
example.context: The area has seen increased violence related to territorial disputes between criminal groups.
example.perpetrator_description: sujetos armados a bordo de un vehiculo

event_type.description: Type of violent incident from the catalogue. Choose the single most specific category that matches.

status.description: Status of the incident as reported.

name.description: Name or identifier for the incident, if any. Usually null.

description.description: Brief description of the violent incident.

context.description: Contextual information: security situation, related events, investigations.

relevance.description: Relevance within the article: 1 = main topic, 2 = secondary, 3 = implicit.

date_range.description: Date or date range when the violent incident occurred. Extract the start datetime and, if present, the end datetime.

casualties.description: Number of casualties (dead, injured, missing), if mentioned.

weapon.description: Weapon or means used, if mentioned. E.g. 'firearm', 'knife', 'explosive'.

victim_description.description: Description of victims (age, gender, occupation), if mentioned.

responding_authority.description: Law enforcement or authority that responded, if mentioned.
